agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration. The design ensures each external system's protocol, data format, and timing constraints are isolated from the core domain, while a unified internal event bus provides the real-time stream that all integrations—and the analytics platform—consume.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Core Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns the canonical domain model and emits all six domain events (OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed). All business logic and invariant enforcement lives here.","boundaries":"Never speaks external protocols. Only produces and consumes domain events via the internal event bus. Has no knowledge of XML, SFTP, DHL APIs, or Kafka topic schemas."},{"name":"Internal Event Bus","responsibility":"Durable, ordered event backbone for all domain events. Serves as the single source of truth for event flow within the platform. Could be an internal Kafka cluster or a lightweight broker like NATS depending on scale.","boundaries":"Carries only canonical domain events in a platform-owned schema. External systems never publish directly to this bus."},{"name":"SAP ERP ACL (Anti-Corruption Layer)","responsibility":"Polls SFTP endpoint every 15 minutes, retrieves XML files, parses and validates them, translates SAP order confirmations into OrderConfirmed domain events, and publishes to the internal event bus. Handles idempotency (tracking processed files), retry on SFTP failures, and XML schema validation.","boundaries":"Knows SAP XML schema and SFTP protocol. Translates inbound only. Outbound to SAP (if needed, e.g., sending OrderPlaced as XML) would be a separate adapter within this ACL."},{"name":"DHL Carrier ACL","responsibility":"Two sub-components: (1) Outbound Adapter — listens for ShipmentCreated events and calls DHL REST API to book shipments, mapping domain model to DHL's API contract. (2) Webhook Receiver — exposes an HTTP endpoint for DHL webhook callbacks, validates signatures, translates DHL status payloads into DeliveryAttempted/DeliveryCompleted/DeliveryFailed events, and publishes to the internal event bus. Must respond within 500ms.","boundaries":"Knows DHL API contract, authentication, and webhook signature format. The webhook receiver is a thin, fast HTTP handler that does minimal processing synchronously — it validates, persists the raw payload to a durable queue, and ACKs within the 500ms budget. Translation and domain event emission happen asynchronously from that queue."},{"name":"Analytics Event Publisher","responsibility":"Subscribes to all domain events on the internal event bus and publishes them to the external Kafka cluster in the schema required by the Analytics Platform. Handles schema translation, Kafka producer retries, and back-pressure.","boundaries":"Knows the Analytics Platform's Kafka topic structure and event schema. Read-only consumer of the internal bus; write-only producer to external Kafka. Never feeds data back into the domain."},{"name":"Webhook Ingestion Buffer","responsibility":"A small durable queue (e.g., Redis Streams, SQS, or an internal Kafka topic) sitting between the DHL webhook HTTP endpoint and the DHL ACL's translation logic. Ensures the 500ms SLA is met by decoupling receipt from processing.","boundaries":"Stores raw DHL webhook payloads. Consumed exclusively by the DHL ACL's async translator."},{"name":"SFTP File Tracker","responsibility":"Persistent store (database table or file manifest) tracking which SAP XML files have been successfully processed. Prevents duplicate processing on re-polls or failure recovery.","boundaries":"Accessed only by the SAP ERP ACL. Stores file names, checksums, and processing timestamps."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Core Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes domain events with schema: {eventId, eventType, aggregateId, timestamp, payload}. Events are immutable and append-only.","coupling_type":"loose — event-driven, schema-coupled only"},{"from":"SAP ERP ACL","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes OrderConfirmed events after translating from SAP XML. Guarantees at-least-once delivery with idempotency keys derived from SAP order IDs.","coupling_type":"loose — ACL isolates SAP schema from domain"},{"from":"SAP ERP ACL","to":"SFTP (SAP)","contract":"Polls SFTP directory every 15 minutes. Expects XML files conforming to SAP IDoc schema. Deletes or moves files to 'processed' directory after successful ingestion.","coupling_type":"tight to SAP protocol — isolated within ACL boundary"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"DHL Carrier ACL (Outbound)","contract":"DHL ACL subscribes to ShipmentCreated events. Translates to DHL REST API booking request. Handles HTTP errors with exponential backoff retry.","coupling_type":"loose — event subscription"},{"from":"DHL Webhook Receiver","to":"Webhook Ingestion Buffer","contract":"Raw DHL JSON payload persisted with receipt timestamp and correlation ID. Synchronous write, must complete in <200ms to leave margin for the 500ms SLA.","coupling_type":"temporal coupling — must be fast and reliable"},{"from":"Webhook Ingestion Buffer","to":"DHL Carrier ACL (Translator)","contract":"ACL translator polls or subscribes to buffer. Translates DHL status codes to domain events (DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed) and publishes to internal event bus.","coupling_type":"loose — async consumption"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"Analytics Event Publisher","contract":"Subscribes to all six event types. Translates to Analytics Platform schema and produces to external Kafka topics partitioned by orderId.","coupling_type":"loose — event-driven, unidirectional"},{"from":"Analytics Event Publisher","to":"Kafka (Analytics Platform)","contract":"Produces events to topics: logistics.orders, logistics.deliveries. Avro or JSON Schema with schema registry. At-least-once semantics.","coupling_type":"tight to Analytics Kafka schema — isolated within publisher boundary"}],"data_flow":"1. OrderPlaced originates in Core Domain → Internal Event Bus. 2. SAP ERP ACL polls SFTP every 15min, ingests XML, emits OrderConfirmed → Internal Event Bus. 3. Core Domain processes OrderConfirmed, may emit ShipmentCreated → Internal Event Bus. 4. DHL Outbound Adapter picks up ShipmentCreated, calls DHL REST API to book shipment. 5. DHL sends webhook callbacks → Webhook Receiver → Ingestion Buffer → DHL ACL Translator → emits DeliveryAttempted/Completed/Failed → Internal Event Bus. 6. Analytics Publisher subscribes to ALL events on Internal Event Bus and forwards to external Kafka in real-time. All paths converge on the Internal Event Bus, making it the single integration backbone.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (DDD)","rationale":"Each external system has a fundamentally different protocol, data format, and timing model. ACLs prevent SAP's XML/batch model and DHL's REST/webhook model from polluting the core domain. The domain never bends to fit an external contract."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture","rationale":"All six domain events are first-class citizens on an internal event bus. This naturally supports the Analytics Platform's real-time streaming requirement and decouples all integration adapters from each other and from the core domain."},{"pattern":"Async Ingestion Buffer (CQRS-lite)","rationale":"The DHL webhook 500ms SLA demands that receipt and processing are decoupled. A small durable buffer between the HTTP endpoint and the translation logic guarantees fast ACKs while preserving reliability."},{"pattern":"Polling Consumer (SAP SFTP)","rationale":"SAP pushes files to SFTP on its schedule (every 15 min). A polling consumer with file-tracking is the natural fit — no real-time requirement on this integration, and it matches SAP's batch-oriented nature."},{"pattern":"Idempotent Consumer","rationale":"At-least-once delivery across SFTP re-polls and webhook retries requires idempotency at every ingestion point. File checksums for SAP, DHL webhook event IDs for carrier callbacks."},{"pattern":"Outbox Pattern (optional enhancement)","rationale":"If the Core Domain Service uses a relational database, the Transactional Outbox pattern ensures domain events are published atomically with state changes — preventing lost events. Recommended for production but not strictly required in the initial design."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: orderId, states: Placed → Confirmed → Shipped)", "OrderLine (value object within Order)"]},{"name":"Shipment & Delivery","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment (root: shipmentId, linked to orderId, states: Created → InTransit → Delivered | Failed)", "DeliveryAttempt (entity within Shipment, tracks each attempt)"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["InboundFileRecord (tracks processed SFTP files, idempotency)"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierBooking (tracks outbound API calls and their status)", "WebhookEvent (raw inbound event with processing status)"]},{"name":"Analytics Publishing","classification":"generic","aggregates":["EventProjection (stateless — no aggregates needed, pure event transformation and forwarding)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Order Management defines what an OrderConfirmed event looks like; ERP Integration conforms to the domain model when translating from SAP XML."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Shipment & Delivery","relationship":"Shared Kernel — both contexts share the orderId identity and a minimal Order reference. Keep the shared surface small."},{"upstream":"Shipment & Delivery","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Shipment context defines ShipmentCreated; Carrier Integration translates to DHL API. Inbound webhooks are translated back into domain events defined by Shipment context."},{"upstream":"Order Management + Shipment & Delivery","downstream":"Analytics Publishing","relationship":"Conformist — Analytics Publishing conforms to whatever events the upstream contexts emit. It has no power to change the domain event schemas."},{"upstream":"SAP ERP (External)","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — SAP's XML/IDoc model is fully translated at the boundary. The ACL protects the domain from SAP's data model and naming conventions."},{"upstream":"DHL (External)","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — DHL's REST contract and webhook payloads are translated at the boundary. Domain never sees DHL-specific status codes or field names."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Single internal event bus as the integration backbone","gains":"Uniform event flow. Analytics gets real-time stream trivially. Easy to add new consumers (e.g., a notification service) without touching existing components. Clear observability — one place to monitor all events.","sacrifices":"Single point of failure if the bus goes down. Additional infrastructure to operate. Slight latency overhead for intra-process communication that could be direct method calls.","alternatives_considered":["Direct point-to-point integration between components — simpler initially but creates a coupling nightmare as integrations grow","Separate event buses per integration — more isolation but fragments the event stream and makes the Analytics requirement harder to fulfill"]},{"decision":"Async buffer for DHL webhooks instead of synchronous processing","gains":"Guarantees 500ms webhook SLA regardless of downstream processing time. Resilient to transient failures in event bus or domain service. Allows independent scaling of webhook receipt vs processing.","sacrifices":"Added infrastructure component. Slight complexity in ensuring exactly-once processing from the buffer. Small additional latency before domain events are emitted.","alternatives_considered":["Synchronous webhook processing — simpler but risks breaching the 500ms SLA under load or when downstream systems are slow","Fire-and-forget with logging — fast but loses reliability guarantees"]},{"decision":"Polling-based SFTP integration rather than push notifications","gains":"Matches SAP's batch file delivery model naturally. Simple to implement and reason about. Easy to handle failures by re-processing files.","sacrifices":"15-minute latency floor for order confirmations. Not suitable if near-real-time confirmation is ever needed.","alternatives_considered":["SFTP file-watch / inotify — more responsive but adds complexity and may not be supported by all SFTP servers","SAP sending events via API — would require changes on SAP's side, which is typically controlled by a different team"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new external integrations","evolution_path":"New systems plug in as new ACL adapters subscribing to or publishing on the internal event bus. No changes to core domain or existing adapters required."},{"area":"Replacing the carrier (DHL → FedEx)","evolution_path":"Build a new Carrier ACL for FedEx. The core domain doesn't change because all carrier-specific details are isolated behind the ACL. Can run both in parallel during migration."},{"area":"Changing the internal event bus technology","evolution_path":"The event bus is accessed through an abstraction (publish/subscribe interface). Swapping NATS for Kafka (or vice versa) requires only adapter changes, not domain changes."},{"area":"Analytics schema evolution","evolution_path":"The Analytics Event Publisher is the only component that knows the external Kafka schema. Schema changes are isolated to this single publisher. Use a schema registry with backward-compatible evolution."},{"area":"Adding real-time notifications (email, SMS)","evolution_path":"Subscribe a new Notification adapter to DeliveryCompleted/DeliveryFailed events on the internal bus. Zero changes to existing components."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Internal domain event schema","reason":"All adapters depend on the canonical event schema. Changes to event structure (e.g., renaming fields, changing payload shape) ripple to every consumer. This is intentional — the domain model should be stable and changes should be deliberate and versioned."},{"area":"DHL webhook endpoint URL and contract","reason":"DHL is configured to POST to a specific URL with a specific payload format. Changing the endpoint requires coordinating with DHL. The webhook receiver's external interface is rigid by necessity."},{"area":"SAP XML file format","reason":"SAP's IDoc XML schema is controlled by the ERP team. The ACL must conform to whatever SAP produces. Changes require cross-team coordination."},{"area":"Kafka topic structure for Analytics","reason":"Once the Analytics Platform is consuming from specific topics with specific schemas, changing topic names or schemas requires coordinated migration."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Internal event bus becomes a single point of failure — if it goes down, all integrations stop","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy the event bus in a highly available cluster configuration (e.g., Kafka with replication factor 3, min.insync.replicas=2). Implement circuit breakers in producers. Add dead-letter queues for failed publications. Monitor lag and availability with alerts."},{"risk":"DHL webhook 500ms SLA breach under high load or ingestion buffer write latency","severity":"high","mitigation":"Keep the webhook receiver extremely thin — validate signature, write raw payload to buffer, return 200. Use an in-memory buffer with async flush if needed. Load test the webhook path independently. Set up p99 latency monitoring with alerts at 400ms."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP file processing fails silently, causing order confirmations to be lost","severity":"high","mitigation":"File tracker records processing status. Alert on files that remain unprocessed after 2 poll cycles. Implement a dead-letter directory for unparseable files. Daily reconciliation job comparing SAP order count vs OrderConfirmed event count."},{"risk":"Duplicate event processing from at-least-once delivery semantics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Idempotency keys on all events (eventId for domain events, SAP order ID + file checksum for SFTP, DHL event ID for webhooks). All consumers must be idempotent — check before processing."},{"risk":"Schema drift between external systems and ACL translators","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Version all external schemas. ACL validates inbound payloads against expected schema before translation. Unknown fields are logged but tolerated (forward compatibility). Breaking changes trigger alerts and require ACL updates."},{"risk":"Analytics Kafka producer back-pressure slows down internal event processing","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Analytics Publisher runs as an independent consumer group with its own consumer offset. Back-pressure in the publisher cannot block the internal event bus or other consumers. If the publisher falls behind, it catches up from the bus's retained events."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Start with the internal event bus (Kafka recommended given the Analytics requirement already mandates Kafka expertise). Build the three ACLs as independent, deployable services. Implement in this order: (1) Internal Event Bus + Core Domain event emission, (2) Analytics Publisher (simplest — validates the bus works end-to-end), (3) DHL Carrier ACL (highest SLA risk — needs early load testing), (4) SAP ERP ACL (lowest urgency — 15-min batch cycle is forgiving). Each ACL should be independently deployable and testable with contract tests against the external system's documented API/schema.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/core/events/domain-events.ts — canonical event type definitions and schemas","src/core/events/event-bus.ts — internal event bus abstraction (publish/subscribe interface)","src/integrations/sap/sftp-poller.ts — SFTP polling loop with file tracking","src/integrations/sap/xml-translator.ts — SAP XML to domain event translation","src/integrations/sap/file-tracker.ts — idempotent file processing registry","src/integrations/dhl/outbound-adapter.ts — ShipmentCreated → DHL REST API caller","src/integrations/dhl/webhook-receiver.ts — thin HTTP handler for DHL callbacks","src/integrations/dhl/webhook-translator.ts — raw DHL payload → domain event translation","src/integrations/dhl/ingestion-buffer.ts — durable buffer between webhook receipt and processing","src/integrations/analytics/event-publisher.ts — internal event → external Kafka producer","src/integrations/analytics/schema-mapper.ts — domain event to Analytics schema translation","infrastructure/docker-compose.yml — local dev setup with Kafka, SFTP mock, Redis","tests/integration/sap-acl.test.ts — contract tests with SAP XML fixtures","tests/integration/dhl-acl.test.ts — contract tests with DHL API/webhook fixtures","tests/integration/analytics-publisher.test.ts — verify event translation and Kafka production","tests/load/webhook-latency.test.ts — load test verifying 500ms SLA"],"patterns_to_follow":"Each ACL follows the same internal structure: Receiver (protocol-specific inbound) → Translator (maps to/from domain) → Publisher/Consumer (event bus interaction). Keep translators pure functions with no side effects for easy unit testing. Use dependency injection for all infrastructure concerns (SFTP client, HTTP client, Kafka producer). Contract tests per integration validate that the ACL correctly handles the external system's documented payloads.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT let external system schemas leak into the core domain model — every field name, status code, and data type from SAP/DHL must be translated at the ACL boundary","DO NOT process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — always buffer first, process async","DO NOT assume SFTP files arrive exactly once — design for duplicate files and partial uploads","DO NOT share Kafka consumer groups between the Analytics Publisher and any internal consumers — they have different SLAs and back-pressure characteristics","DO NOT build a generic 'integration framework' upfront — each ACL has different enough concerns that premature abstraction will create complexity without value","DO NOT skip idempotency — at-least-once delivery means every consumer must handle duplicates gracefully"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["ACL pattern per external system is clearly the right call — three fundamentally different protocols and data models","Event-driven backbone is well-suited given the Analytics real-time streaming requirement already exists","Async buffer for DHL webhooks is necessary to meet the 500ms SLA","Polling for SFTP is the natural and correct pattern for batch file delivery"],"low_confidence_areas":["Specific event bus technology choice (Kafka vs NATS vs internal) depends on existing infrastructure and team expertise — assumed Kafka given Analytics requirement","Whether the Outbox pattern is needed depends on the Core Domain's persistence layer — cannot determine without knowing the database choice","Exact Kafka topic partitioning strategy for Analytics depends on their consumption patterns and query requirements"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform owns the internal event bus infrastructure","SAP cannot be modified to push events via API — SFTP is the only option","DHL webhook payloads include a unique event ID usable for idempotency","The Analytics Platform team has defined or will define their Kafka topic schema","The internal event bus can retain events long enough for Analytics Publisher to catch up after outages"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration. The design ensures each external system's protocol, data format, and timing constraints are isolated from the core domain, while a unified internal event bus provides the real-time stream that all integrations—and the analytics p
